activity_summary: Read-only review of the dashboard refresh workflow - leakage_dashboard.html, dashboard_refresh_prompt.md and refresh_dashboard.py. Confirmed the architecture: PostgreSQL via MCP to SQL to dashboardData, replacing ONLY the WLSP_DATA_START/END block and never rewriting HTML/CSS/JS. Found the prompt SQL correct (Amazon-only, COUNT DISTINCT, L5 global, UNATTRIBUTED excluded, no breakdown). Flagged two open items: the prompt caps l1/l2 to top-60 which limits the row-counting cards, and refresh_dashboard.py does not verify the non-data HTML is byte-for-byte unchanged.
evidence_refs: file mtimes 2026-06-26; single WLSP_DATA_START/END block; prompt Amazon filters + COUNT(DISTINCT asin); py JSON/keys/forbidden/marketplace guards
reusable_pattern: Refresh = data-block-only replace; template never auto-rewritten; validate JSON+keys+counts+forbidden-tables+marketplace before accepting
validation_rule: Refresh must change ONLY dashboardData; HTML/CSS/JS layout must never be auto-rewritten
gap_or_risk: Prompt l1/l2 top-60 cap vs row-counting cards; refresh_dashboard.py lacks byte-for-byte non-data preservation + atomic write
next_action: Decide card meaning (true total vs rows-shown); add byte-for-byte guard + atomic write to refresh_dashboard.py

activity_title: Locked row-counting dashboard design and verified L5 account-independence
activity_summary: Locked the final sidebar-count design - counts derived live from the l1-l4 detail rows via viewMatch (account AND PH filter); All accounts equals LEDSone plus DCVoltage; each PH rolls up into its account; L5 (Margin Trend) is global/per-PH and excluded from flagged totals; NO account_breakdown (the rows already carry account and ph, so per-account PH counts come from filtering the rows). Verified Margin Trend is correctly account-independent - renderL5 filters by PH and search only, not account - confirmed by design, not a bug.
evidence_refs: countFor=rowsFor(k).filter(viewMatch).length; FLAG_KEYS=l1-l4; totalFlagged excludes l5; renderL5 has no acctFilter; l5 rows carry no account field
reusable_pattern: Count live from detail rows (each row carries account+ph) so All == sum of accounts and PH rolls into account; L5 global, never account-split; breakdown method unnecessary
validation_rule: Sidebar counts come from l1-l4 rows; All == LEDSone + DCVoltage; L5 excluded from flagged totals and never filtered by account
gap_or_risk: Row-counting cards display the embedded-row count; for true totals the embedded l1/l2 must be uncapped
next_action: If cards must show true totals, embed l1/l2 in full via the prompt

activity_summary: Fixed the unreadable count on the selected (blue) account row. Root cause was a CSS specificity tie - .acct-opt .ao-ct.oth{color:var(--muted)} (3 classes) overrode .acct-opt.on .ao-ct{color:#fff} (3 classes) by source order, so the selected-row count rendered muted-grey on blue. Raised the selected-state rule to 4-class specificity (.acct-opt.on .ao-ct.led/.dcv/.oth) so the count always renders white on the selected row. Applied to leakage_dashboard.html and index.html. Presentation only - no JS, data, counts or business logic changed.
evidence_refs: CSS rule .acct-opt.on .ao-ct.oth{color:#fff} present in both files; data block unchanged (counts 125/212/17/33/9)
reusable_pattern: Selected-state badge colour must out-specify any colour-variant rule (4 classes > 3) so it stays readable
validation_rule: WLSP-VAL-07 - selected sidebar count must remain readable on the highlighted row
